SDM with AdaBoost

On this page

Using AdaBoost ​

In this vignette, we will see how we can use AdaBoost to turn a mediocre model into a less mediocre one.

julia
using SpeciesDistributionToolkit
const SDT = SpeciesDistributionToolkit
using CairoMakie
julia
POL = getpolygon(PolygonData(OpenStreetMap, Places), place="Paraguay")
presences = GBIF.download("10.15468/dl.d3cxpr")
Occurrences(Occurrence[Occurrence("Akodon montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-54.795118, -25.49267), missing), Occurrence("Akodon montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-55.472029, -26.697482), missing), missing), Occurrence("Akodon montensis", true, (-55.7737, -26.2689), missing), Occurrence("Akodon montensis", true, (-55.7887, -26.2812), missing), Occurrence("Akodon (Akodon) montensis Thomas, 1913", Occurrence("Akodon (Akodon) montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-56.9167, -25.6667), missing), Occurrence("Akodon (Akodon) montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-56.9167, -25.6667), missing), Occurrence("Akodon (Akodon) montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-56.9167, -25.6667), missing), Occurrence("Akodon (Akodon) montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-56.9167, -25.6667), missing), Occurrence("Akodon (Akodon) montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-56.9167, -25.6667), missing), Occurrence("Akodon (Akodon) montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-56.9167, -25.6667), missing), Occurrence("Akodon (Akodon) montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-56.9167, -25.6667), missing), Occurrence("Akodon (Akodon) montensis Thomas, 1913", true, (-56.9167, -25.6667), missing)])

get the spatial data

julia
bio_vars = 1:19
provider = RasterData(CHELSA2, BioClim)
L = SDMLayer{Float32}[
    SDMLayer(
        provider;
        layer = x,
        SDT.boundingbox(POL; padding=0.0)...,
    ) for x in bio_vars
];

mask

julia
mask!(L, POL);

generate PA

julia
presencelayer = mask(first(L), presences)
background = pseudoabsencemask(DistanceToEvent, presencelayer)
bgpoints = backgroundpoints(nodata(background, d -> (d < 20)|(d > 300)), 2sum(presencelayer))
🗺️  A 999 × 1007 layer with 244838 Bool cells
   Projection: +proj=longlat +datum=WGS84 +no_defs

train a weak learner (bioclim)

The BIOCLIM model (Booth et al., 2014) is one of the first SDM that was ever published. Because it is a climate envelope model (locations within the extrema of environmental variables for observed presences are assumed suitable, more or less), it does not usually give really good predictions. BIOCLIM underfits, and for this reason it is a good instance of a weak learner that we will attempt to improve through boosting.

julia
sdm = SDM(RawData, NaiveBayes, L, presencelayer, bgpoints)
variables!(sdm, BackwardSelection)
RawData → NaiveBayes → P(x) ≥ 0.005

make initial prediction with this model

julia
prd = predict(sdm, L; threshold = false)
🗺️  A 999 × 1007 layer with 508483 Float64 cells
   Projection: +proj=longlat +datum=WGS84 +no_defs

plot now

Code for the figure
julia
fg, ax, pl = heatmap(prd; colormap = :tempo)
ax.aspect = DataAspect()
hidedecorations!(ax)
hidespines!(ax)
lines!(ax, POL, color=:grey20)
scatter!(ax, presencelayer; color = :orange, markersize=4)
Colorbar(fg[1, 2], pl)

now setup the boosting

julia
bst = AdaBoost(sdm; iterations = 40)
train!(bst)
AdaBoost {RawData → NaiveBayes → P(x) ≥ 0.005} × 40 iterations

check the number of iterations and effect on weight

julia
scatterlines(bst.weights, color=:black)
julia
brd = predict(bst, L)
🗺️  A 999 × 1007 layer with 508483 Float64 cells
   Projection: +proj=longlat +datum=WGS84 +no_defs

fig-boosted-map

julia
fg, ax, pl = heatmap(brd; colormap = :tempo, colorrange=(0, 1))
ax.aspect = DataAspect()
hidedecorations!(ax)
hidespines!(ax)
lines!(ax, POL, color=:grey20)
scatter!(ax, presencelayer; color = :orange, markersize=5)
Colorbar(fg[1, 2], pl, height=Relative(0.6))

difference of quantiles

fig-quantile-diff

julia
fg, ax, pl = heatmap(quantize(brd) - quantize(prd); colormap = Reverse(:balance), colorrange=(-0.15, 0.15))
ax.aspect = DataAspect()
hidedecorations!(ax)
hidespines!(ax)
lines!(ax, POL, color=:grey20)
Colorbar(fg[1, 2], pl, height=Relative(0.6))
julia
v = rand(variables(sdm))
18

fig-response-boosted

julia
lines(partialresponse(bst, v)...)
lines!(partialresponse(sdm, v; threshold=false)...)
julia
yhat = predict(bst; threshold=false)

T = LinRange(0.0, 1.0, 1000)
C = zeros(ConfusionMatrix, length(T))

for i in eachindex(T)
    C[i] = ConfusionMatrix(yhat, labels(bst), T[i])
end

Code for the figure
julia
lines(T, mcc.(C))
julia
thr = T[last(findmax(mcc.(C)))]

rbst = brd .>= thr
rsdm = predict(sdm, L)
🗺️  A 999 × 1007 layer with 508483 Bool cells
   Projection: +proj=longlat +datum=WGS84 +no_defs

fig-gainloss-boosting

julia
fg, ax, pl = heatmap(gainloss(rsdm, rbst); colormap = [:firebrick, :tan, :black], colorrange=(-1, 1))
ax.aspect = DataAspect()
hidedecorations!(ax)
hidespines!(ax)
contour!(rbst, color=:firebrick, linewidth=0.5)
lines!(ax, POL, color=:grey20)
scatter!(ax, presences)
scatter!(ax, bgpoints)
